title: High Direct GP
definition: Direct GP productivity ranks in the top tier.
example: Retain SKU with strongest direct contribution.

title: High Halo GP
definition: Cross-category halo contribution materially lifts total GP.
example: Keep diaper SKU that pulls wipes and cream.

title: Core Coverage
definition: SKU is required to cover a core need state.
example: Protect NB tape entry to avoid coverage gap.

title: KVI Protection
definition: KVI or image-sensitive SKU receives a penalty if removed.

title: Duplicate Rationalization
definition: SKU removed because same need state already has stronger alternatives.
example: Delist weak duplicate mid-tier size M tape.

title: Shelf Capacity
definition: Decision is constrained by available shelf width.
example: Facings capped due to shelf limit.

title: Brand Cap Guardrail
definition: Brand facings share approached the cap rule.
example: No more Brand A facings allowed.

title: OSA Risk Alert
definition: Recommended facings are raised to reduce stockout risk.
example: Add one facing for high run-rate SKU with low stock cover.

title: Manual Override
definition: Decision changed by approved operator override.
example: Force keep a regional hero SKU.

title: Local Search Upgrade
definition: Heuristic swap improved total objective versus initial greedy selection.
example: Replace low-score SKU with higher-value peer.

title: Forced Delist
definition: Approved override explicitly delisted the SKU.

title: Forced Keep
definition: Approved override explicitly retained the SKU.
example: Buyer requested keep despite low score.
